title: 'We are walking a fine line': Incurably ill patients on France's new assisted-dying law
subhed: A law creating a right to aid in dying was adopted by French lawamkers on Wednesday. Le Monde spoke with people facing serious illness about how the choice weighs on their lives.

René Obermann, chairman of Airbus: 'We must put an end to industrial nationalism in defense'
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde and Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung, the chair of the board of the European aerospace and defense group expressed concern about the future of French-German cooperation, which has been undermined by the cancellation in June of the FCAS, the next-generation fighter jet project.

title: Christopher Nolan struggles to match Homer in 'The Odyssey'
subhed: In his search for an original aesthetic capable of capturing the epic masterpiece of antiquity, the filmmaker opts for grandeur, with a prestigious cast and vast resources, but fails to convince.

title: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup 2026 final
subhed: Argentina will have a chance to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups after Lionel Messi, 39, once again dragged his team to victory.

title: 2026 World Cup: How France's American dream built up speed – and crashed
subhed: France fell hard to Spain on Tuesday in the semifinals of the football tournament, marking a cruel and unexpected ending for Didier Deschamps' squad, who had delighted supporters since the start of the competition.

From Paris to Nice, Macron marks his final Bastille Day with a series of commemorations
subhed: For his final national holiday as president, Emmanuel Macron presided over an unprecedented military parade and paid solemn tribute to the victims of the Nice terror attack.

title: Ukraine expands attacks in Sea of Azov, a crucial maritime corridor for Russia
subhed: After choking off Crimea, which Moscow has occupied since 2014, Ukrainian drones are waging a relentless naval campaign against Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet,' which has been accused of evading sanctions. This has brought the traffic of these vessels on the Sea of Azov to a near halt.

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhed: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.
